title: General Bereavement and Grief Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > General Bereavement and Grief Support Groups 14 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COLUMBIA MEMORIAL HOSPITAL GRIEF SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides grief support services, including a monthly drop-in group and a once-a-week eight-week grief support program. 2111 Exchange Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by support group Email (503) 338-6230 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES DOUGY CENTER EAST PEER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-ended peer support groups for those grieving a death, as well as groups for individuals living with advanced serious illness. Services are available in-person and online. Also offers specialized information and referral on these topics. 3909 SE 52nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Children and teens ages 3-18 and young adults ages 19-35 and their families who have experienced the death of a parent, primary caregiver, or sibling Hours: Support group times vary Email (503) 775-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES DOUGY CENTER SOUTH PEER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-ended peer support groups for those grieving a death, as well as groups for individuals living with advanced serious illness. Services are available in-person and online. Also offers specialized information and referral on these topics. 350 SE 13th Avenue Canby, OR 97013 Eligibility: Children and teens ages 3-18 and young adults ages 19-35 and their families who have experienced the death of a parent, primary caregiver, or sibling Hours: Support group times vary Email (503) 775-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES DOUGY CENTER WEST PEER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-ended peer support groups for those grieving a death, as well as groups for individuals living with advanced serious illness. Services are available in-person and online. Also offers specialized information and referral on these topics. 13565 SW Walker Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Children and teens ages 3-18 and young adults ages 19-35 and their families who have experienced the death of a parent, primary caregiver, or sibling Hours: Support group times vary Email (503) 775-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRIEFSHARE SUPPORT GROUP LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a web resource to connect individuals to local bereavement support groups. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by location Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING HOPE CHURCH LIVING FREE MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Christian faith-based support groups and classes for individuals experiencing addiction to alcohol, drugs, food, or pornography, as well as for those dealing with anger, depression, grief, and divorce. Offers Beyond the Walls, a class for families with loved ones who are incarcerated. Child care is available for support groups and classes. Also provides a community meal. Worship services are offered but are not required for participation. 2711 NE Andresen Road Vancouver, WA 98661 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Community meal: Tuesday 5:30pm -Support groups: 7:20pm, support group times vary -Worship service: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:20pm Email (360) 944-3905 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE OPEN COMMUNITY CIRCLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides grief support in a group setting. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 1st and 3rd Thursdays of the month 6pm-7:30pm Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE GRIEF TRAVELERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an ongoing 4 week series for 6-10 participants with weekly 60 minute group sessions. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE INDIVIDUAL GRIEF COUNSELING SESSIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual counseling sessions. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE GRIEF JOURNEY EXTENDED PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers extended grief and bereavement counseling programs two times a year in the spring and fall. Sessions run for 12 weeks up to 12 months depending on the needs of the group. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING UNDONE TEEN GROUP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe place for teenagers to come and deal with various issues related to grief. 33 NW Louisiana Avenue Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Ages 11-17. Participants must commit to four weeks of the program. Some age exceptions are permitted depending on the maturity of the child. Hours: Wednesday 3pm-4pm Email (541) 223-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MT HOOD HOSPICE GRIEF SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides conversation and education groups designed to foster social connection, expand understanding, and develop coping skills for grieving a loss due to death. Groups are focused on grief and complicated circumstances such as substance use, abuse, mental health challenges, or strained relationships with the deceased. 39085 Pioneer Boulevard Suite 1018 Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Adults who have experienced the loss of an important person or are caring for a loved one with a terminal diagnosis Hours: Varies by group Email (503) 668-5545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEACEHEALTH URGENT CARE UNIVERSITY DISTRICT COURAGEOUS KIDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a grief support group focused on therapeutic art activities and educational resources. Support groups for parents and caregivers run at the same time as groups for children and youth, allowing families to receive help together. 1200 Hilyard Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Children, youth, and their caregivers who have experienced the loss of a friend, parent, sibling, or loved one Hours: Varies by session Email (458) 205-7474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEERGALAXY PEERGALAXY ONLINE COMMUNITY MEETINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides online community meetings to discuss various positive topics. These topics include life skills, self-reliance, creative expression, spiritual growth, wellness, peer support, and healing and recovery from mental health challenges, addictions, trauma, and grief. Meetings occur weekly and/or seasonally on the HeyPeers platform, usually on most days. Each meeting series typically lasts 10 weeks, although some may be longer or shorter, and each session generally lasts for 1 hour. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email Visit Website More Details SALEM ALLIANCE CHURCH GRIEF SUPPORT GROUP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a grief and loss support group to assist the healing process. 555 Gaines Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Those who are grieving Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 581-2129 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAMARITAN HEALTH SERVICES SAMARITAN CENTER FOR HEALTH EDUCATION CENTER FOR HEALTH EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides health and wellness classes, support groups, CPR classes, and resources that focus on prevention and managing existing conditions. Also offers wellness consultations. 740 SW 9th Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 574-4952 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE GRIEF HOUSE GRIEF SUPPORT GATHERINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-person and virtual community-led grief support events for different types of loss including the death of a loved one, job loss, pet loss, divorce or estrangement, and other life changes. 7906 N Fessenden Street Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Ages 18 and older who are experiencing grief Hours: Hours vary by event Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details WESTSIDE CHURCH GRIEF SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 13 week support group for those going through the process of grieving. 2051 NW Shelvin Park Road Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA MEMORIAL HOSPITAL GRIEF SUPPORT DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES PEER SUPPORT GROUPS DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES PEER SUPPORT GROUPS DOUGY CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN AND FAMILIES PEER SUPPORT GROUPS LIVING UNDONE OPEN COMMUNITY CIRCLES MT HOOD HOSPICE GRIEF SUPPORT GROUPS PEACEHEALTH COURAGEOUS KIDS SALEM ALLIANCE CHURCH GRIEF SUPPORT GROUP SAMARITAN HEALTH SERVICES CENTER FOR HEALTH EDUCATION THE GRIEF HOUSE GRIEF SUPPORT GATHERINGS WESTSIDE CHURCH GRIEF SHARE
